name: OpenTTD
short_description: OpenTTD is a business simulation game in which players earn money by transporting passengers and cargo via road, rail, water, and air. It is an open-source remake and expansion of the 1995 Chris Sawyer video game Transport Tycoon Deluxe.
detailed_description:
OpenTTD is a business simulation game in which players earn money by transporting passengers and cargo via road, rail, water, and air. It is an open-source remake and expansion of the 1995 Chris Sawyer video game Transport Tycoon Deluxe.

Build


Connect industries and cities on procedurally-generated maps of up to 4096 x 4096 tiles by building a network of roads, railways, docks, and airports. Navigate dense city streets, cross oceans and rivers, and conquer high mountains in one of four climates.

Transport


Work alone or with friends to complete production chains and transport finished goods to towns. Assemble a fleet of hundreds of vehicles, transferring cargo as needed in an interconnected, multimodal network with optional cargo destinations.

Upgrade


Keep up with technological improvements through the years as towns grow; new modes of transport are invented; and industries appear, change production, or fail. Expand stations and build network capacity to keep up with demand and avoid delays.

Extend


Customize your OpenTTD experience via community-made mods downloaded through an in-game content service, including real-world vehicles, new industry sets, custom scenarios, and more.

Free and open-source


OpenTTD is free and open-source software, licensed under the GNU General Public License version 2.0. OpenTTD is under ongoing development. OpenTTD is not an abbreviation of anything.

supported_languages: English, French, Italian, German, Spanish - Spain, Arabic, Bulgarian, Czech, Danish, Dutch, Finnish, Greek, Hungarian, Japanese, Korean, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese - Portugal, Portuguese - Brazil, Romanian, Russian, Simplified Chinese, Spanish - Latin America, Swedish, Thai, Traditional Chinese, Turkish, Ukrainian, Vietnamese

reviews.review_text: Absolutely recommended if you're a fan of the the original. This community version has far more work put into it than the "official" one released by Atari.

OpenTTD is a fantastic game I have spent many hours on with friends. If you want to play for free go to https://www.openttd.org/ and download the game.

However Atari has recently forced by their own admission the maintainers of this game to make it usable on steam only with a bundle of the original game because it would harm the "commercial interests" of Atari. Without the maintainers continued effort this game would not be nearly as popular as it is and not nearly as well featured and fleshed out.

Do not support these business practices and download the game from https://www.openttd.org/

I got this because it was a free and incredibly fun game, then it got locked behind a paywall.
You can get it for free on their website

https://www.openttd.org/

It is a good version of the original TTD. I still miss the old games and this one lets me relive those days. Thank you for this wonderful port. Still feels odd to use the legacy style UI, but it was real fun not being bogged down by the ultra details of modern day games.
I dont understand how it works
